title: Word of a Living Hearth
intro: A trader with frightened eyes brings you more than goods: word of another living hearth, a hold to the south that keeps its own Ember behind a stone door and a wary Circle. "They know your fire burns too," he murmurs. "They wonder if you'll come as a friend — or as the one who takes their fire for your own." It is a season's road, there and back, and it would strip two Wizards from the Circle while they walk it.

advisors.text: This is how the Flame comes home, Archon — hearth by hearth, thread by thread. Send an embassy, and send it in good faith. Two shards tended together burn nearer to whole.
Or send an embassy that keeps its options open. If they will treat, treat. If they are weak, we now know where a second Ember sleeps, and who guards it.

choices.label: Send a pilgrimage to the rival hearth

win.text: You choose who will carry Runehold's name south, and set them on the long road to a stranger's fire. Whether they come home with an ally or an Ember, only the season will tell.
win.saga: You sent a pilgrimage south to the rival hearth that keeps its own living Ember.

choices.label: Leave the other hearth to its own fire

win.text: You send the trader on with a full belly and no answer. Let the southern hold keep its fire and its secrets; Runehold has road enough to walk without adding another.
win.saga: You let word of the rival living hearth pass, leaving the southern hold to its own fire.
